finding.recommendation: Hoist the canonical terminal journal taxonomy into deep-loop-runtime, but only as a shared lifecycle contract for the six stopReason values and four sessionOutcome values with mode-extensible event and gate schemas. Do not add an improvement loopType to the runtime coverage graph or convergence.cjs in this migration; keep deep-improvement convergence mode-local because its plateau and trajectory logic is config-driven score/candidate state, not graph-substrate saturation. Use adapters so existing per-mode artifacts remain byte-identical while terminal summaries map onto the runtime contract.
key_tradeoffs: Shared terminal taxonomy reduces drift, but event types and legal-stop gates must remain mode-specific to avoid flattening behavior.
Keeping improvement convergence divergent preserves byte-identical behavior, but postpones any future unified convergence engine for candidate-score loops.
Mapping userPaused to manualStop in shared summaries preserves the six-value enum, but legacy paused-run state logs may need to retain userPaused for artifact parity.
risks: A strict hoisted validator will reject current research/review userPaused terminal reasons unless an adapter maps them to manualStop.
Model-benchmark currently emits legal_stop_evaluated without gateResults, which conflicts with the current improvement-journal validator.
Adding improvement to runtime coverage-graph LoopType would require a SQLite schema/type/signal migration and would violate the structure-only migration intent.
The improvement mutation-coverage.json name can be confused with runtime coverage-graph SQLite even though they are different substrates.
depends_on: Q-CMD 5/6 command surface and loop-host decision
Q-IMPROVE 12 Lane C and Lane D fit
open_questions: Are paused-run artifacts included in the byte-identical parity fixture set?
Should legacy mode-local logs retain userPaused while shared terminal summaries normalize to manualStop?
Should Lane D expose its external stop taxonomy through the same runtime terminal mapper or remain adapter-only until its contract version is bumped?
